danger_signals.visual_form: Larger sphere-constructed entities with distinct color schemes and animal/mystical forms
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating threat level; progression markers in tournament ladder

danger_signals.visual_form: Rapid sphere reconfiguration, charging attacks, extended limbs
Sphere integrity degradation (inferred from fighting game conventions)
Lamprey genie transforming into El Ballz (bull), Spike (scorpion), Byte Viper (snake)
danger_signals.meaning: Unpredictability and escalated threat; boss repositioning for new attack patterns

safety_signals.visual_form: Character poses and taunts after winning exchanges
Character coloring scheme change (Red, Green, Blue, Black belts from boss defeats)
safety_signals.meaning: Progression safety; player competency validation through cosmetic upgrade
Stable game state; ready for next interaction

faction_indicators.color_identity: Varies by selected fighter; transforms based on defeated boss belt color
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Individual character silhouette/archetype (clown, bodybuilder, dancer, etc.)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Arena positioning (player side implied by tournament bracket)

faction_indicators.color_identity: Distinct from player palette; escalates through tournament (Guggler→Bounder→T-Wrecks→Lamprey→Jester)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Animal/creature type identity (ostrich, kangaroo, dinosaur, genie, jester)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Lamprey uniquely displays transformation variants (bull, scorpion, snake forms)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Boss arena presentation; final boss Jester features black-and-white outfit, ball juggling

faction_indicators.color_identity: Diverse character-specific palettes reflecting archetype (e.g., Divine ballerina, Bruiser bodybuilder)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Character selection screen positioning (regional naming variations: US vs. JP version differentiation)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss opponent tier escalation; character sphere complexity and size scaling
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Opponent AI difficulty increase; more aggressive attack patterns and special abilities (Jester disassembly, T-Wrecks ground shockwaves)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Earned belt color applied to player character's sphere coloring scheme (Red→Green→Blue→Black progression)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Visual reward feedback; player cosmetic advancement without mechanical change

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Black-and-white aesthetic contrast; juggling ball visual motif; disassembly/reassembly animation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Multi-phase combat; defeat triggers reassembly for second encounter; grants access to boss playability upon ultimate defeat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sphere-based construction allows identical visual readability from all camera angles; no faction-specific model variants
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat effectiveness depends purely on character archetype abilities (Tsunami's leap attack, Yoko's wind break, Divine's spanking), not visual faction markers

environmental_storytelling.example: Kronk (caveman with club), Tsunami (sumo wrestler), Guggler (ostrich boss) — occupational/species identity communicates role and expected move-set without UI exposition

Tournament ladder moves from animal fighters (Guggler, Bounder) to mythical/transcendent (T-Wrecks dinosaur, Lamprey genie) to abstract trickster (Jester), suggesting narrative shift from naturalistic to supernatural threat
Belt color system (Red→Green→Blue→Black) creates visible progression record on player character, serving as persistent world-state indicator of tournament advancement
Jester's opening presentation (juggling balls) and final phase behavior (self-disassembly/reassembly) communicates trickster nature and control over the arena itself

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Air jumps, trick attacks; humorous fighter archetype
Breaks wind (attack), gangs up on opponent, slaps repeatedly
Leaps on top of opponents; weight-based damage
Pecks at opponents, tosses with beak, jump-kick ability
Bites opponents in jaws, creates ground shockwaves with tail; size-based intimidation
Swift attacks, magical powers, transformation into El Ballz (bull), Spike (scorpion), Byte Viper (snake)
Juggling ball animations, strong physical attacks, high damage output
Reassembles from disassembly; returns for rematch with renewed threat
Twirls gracefully, occasionally gives spanking attack; only female fighter archetype
Charges directly at opponents; aggression-focused strategy

notes: Ballz 3D uses sphere-based character construction as universal visual language, eliminating faction-specific model variants. World-state communication relies heavily on tournament progression (boss tier escalation), character archetype visual identity (profession/species), and cosmetic belt rewards rather than environmental or faction-specific signaling. The game lacks traditional political faction systems; instead, narrative progression is communicated through boss encounter sequencing and final boss Jester's supernatural transformation mechanics. Regional differences (US vs. JP) affect menu presentation and character naming but not core visual communication of danger/safety/world-state. T3 data tier limits precision; behavioral details inferred from character descriptions and documented gameplay mechanics.
